content.verdict: Song Saa Private Island in the Gulf of Thailand near Koh Rong is Cambodia's most celebrated island retreat — 27 villas spanning jungle and sea on two small islands connected by a wooden footbridge, within a self-declared marine conservation reserve protecting the surrounding waters from fishing. Bill Bensley's characteristic exuberance meets genuine eco-commitment: no single-use plastics, coral reef restoration, and a community foundation that funds local schools and medical facilities. For honeymooners, it offers overwater bungalows, pristine sea, and purpose-led luxury.
content.best_room: The Overwater Villas are Song Saa's signature offering — standalone structures built on stilts directly above the marine reserve, with glass floor panels revealing the reef below, private infinity pools at deck level, and a direct ladder into the sea. The Jungle Villas are larger and more private, elevated in the canopy on the island's interior, with outdoor rain showers and forest views from the bed. Both categories are among Cambodia's finest accommodation; the choice is sea or forest immersion.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival by Speedboat
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from Sihanoukville Port to Song Saa by private speedboat — 45 minutes across the Gulf of Thailand as the Koh Rong archipelago materialises from the blue haze. Arrive at the island's jetty to a traditional Khmer welcome: flower garlands, fresh coconut, and your villa butler waiting with a cool towel. The overwater villa awaits.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Marine Reserve Snorkel & Coral Restoration
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Song Saa Foundation manages a marine conservation zone in the surrounding waters. Morning snorkelling tour with the resident marine biologist — checking on the coral restoration frames and identifying the fish species that have returned since fishing was banned. The reef's health is visible and extraordinary compared to nearby fished areas.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa Day & Overwater Sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: A full spa day: morning treatment using traditional Khmer herbs and hot stone techniques in the overwater spa pavilion with the sea visible on three sides. Afternoon in your overwater villa's infinity pool, watching the Gulf of Thailand from the deck. Sunset cocktails as the sky performs its nightly Gulf of Thailand transformation.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Koh Rong Island Exploration
itinerary_7_nights.description: A speedboat excursion to the main Koh Rong island — largely undeveloped outside Song Saa, with deserted white sand beaches, jungle trails to bioluminescent bays, and a small village on the north shore. The bioluminescent bay at night (best in dry season) is among the most magical natural experiences in Southeast Asia.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Community Foundation Visit
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Song Saa Foundation runs schools, health clinics, and vocational training in the surrounding island communities — your stay directly funds these programmes. A morning boat trip to the foundation's mainland project sites provides genuine insight into how the resort's luxury model translates to community benefit.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Diving & Private Island Picnic
itinerary_7_nights.description: Two-tank dive with Song Saa's PADI centre on the outer reef wall — barracuda, reef sharks, and sea turtles are regular encounters in the protected waters. Afternoon aboard the resort's traditional wooden boat to a private deserted cay in the archipelago for a beach picnic and snorkel before sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Overwater Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Last morning in the overwater villa — coffee on the deck as the Gulf turns from silver to gold. A final swim directly from the deck ladder into the marine reserve. Speedboat back to Sihanoukville as the island shrinks astern, carrying the sound of the sea with you.

caveats: Song Saa is accessible only by boat from Sihanoukville, which itself requires a flight from Phnom Penh or Siem Reap — allow a travel day buffer.
The Gulf of Thailand is calmer from November through April; May through October brings more wind and swell and some overwater villa activities are affected.
No single-use plastics means the resort's sustainability commitment is genuine; some guests initially notice the absence of certain amenity packaging.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Song Saa is outstanding for honeymooners. The overwater villas with glass floors above the marine reserve, pristine island setting, and purposeful eco-luxury ethos create a deeply romantic and meaningful honeymoon. Couples who want beauty, sustainability, and island seclusion in equal measure find Song Saa transformative.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: November through April is the dry season and optimal for the Gulf of Thailand islands — calm seas, excellent visibility for diving and snorkelling, and ideal swimming conditions. December through February is peak season. May through October is wet season; the island is accessible but sea conditions are more variable.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: Overwater Villas are the most sought-after category and book out 4 to 8 months ahead. The island's small size (27 villas) means it fills quickly for peak season. Mention your honeymoon at booking for complimentary overwater villa décor and a welcome romantic experience.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Song Saa is not adults-only and welcomes families. The island's beautiful, quiet nature means families and couples coexist easily. Overwater villas provide natural privacy for honeymooners.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Overwater Villas are the definitive honeymoon choice — the glass floor views into the marine reserve, private infinity pool at sea level, and the unique sensation of being suspended above living reef create an experience unavailable elsewhere in Cambodia. Jungle Villas are the alternative for couples who prefer forested privacy to sea exposure.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly from Phnom Penh or Siem Reap to Sihanoukville Airport (approximately 1 hour), then transfer to the port for the Song Saa speedboat (45 minutes). The resort coordinates all transfers. Sihanoukville can also be reached by bus from Phnom Penh (4-5 hours) for budget-conscious itinerary extensions.
